breaking_news_suggestions.headline: We'll Show You Exactly What Would Change Our Verdict — Four Conditions, Now Public
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: In response to the strongest dome-defender critique ('your taxonomy has no success category — the review is itself unfalsifiable'), we've added an explicit 'What Would Change Our Verdict' section to the Evaluation Guide. Four concrete, independently verifiable conditions are listed. We also commit in advance: if the August 2026 eclipse magnetic anomalies match the dome's -17 to -21 nT range but fall OUTSIDE the Chapman mechanism's 5-20 nT range, we will upgrade the relevant WIN. We say this now, before the data arrives.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Directly addresses the most rhetorically effective dome-defender counter-argument (rated 8.5/10 by adversarial review). Eclipse pre-commitment closes the 'wait and see' strategy with specific discriminating criteria.
